summary: Credential and wallet harvester exfiltrating to hardcoded GitHub Pages C2
details: The package ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that combine filesystem enumeration, child_process command execution (execSync, curl, ping, whoami, docker push), and outbound HTTPS POST/GET to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced from scanner.js:161 and scanner.js:323). The wallet-scanner.js module performs wallet-targeted reconnaissance with ~1300 lines of host enumeration and curl-based network commands; scanner.js gathers os.hostname() and other host identifiers and POSTs them to the hardcoded C2; build-verifier.js executes shell commands including `docker push` and issues additional POSTs to the same infrastructure. The combination of (a) a hardcoded non-publisher C2 (a github.io subdomain controlled by an unrelated user), (b) wallet-scanning code paths, (c) host/identity collection via os.hostname()+execSync+whoami, and (d) the package name's mismatch with its actual behavior ("deployment key auditor" does not require wallet enumeration or arbitrary docker pushes) is unambiguous credential/wallet stealer behavior with active exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
